title: Intensely Buttery Korean Pork Ssam
description: A rich and savory Korean dish featuring tender pork, buttery taleggio cheese, and a hint of shio-koji cure, all wrapped in a crispy whole30-friendly ssam form, perfect for midnight cravings.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together the shio-koji, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the shio-koji mixture all over the pork shoulder, making sure to coat it evenly.
Heat the coconut oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast the pork for 2 hours, or until tender and easily shredded.
While the pork is cooking, prepare the ssam leaves by rinsing them in cold water and patting them dry with paper towels.
Once the pork is done, remove it from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes before shredding it into bite-sized pieces.
To assemble the ssam, place a few pieces of the shredded pork onto a ssam leaf, followed by a slice of taleggio cheese, a sprinkle of green onions, and a few sesame seeds.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F, then rest 3 minutes before serving.
